<!--
Copyright (c) 2026 Dhruv Azad. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Dhruv Azad
-->

---
title: "Bisection Method"
page-layout: full
---

```{=html}
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mathjs@13.2.0/lib/browser/math.js"></script>
<script src="https://cdn.plot.ly/plotly-2.35.2.min.js"></script>
```

Visualize how the bisection method repeatedly halves an interval containing a sign change. Choose a function and starting bracket, then step through the narrowing intervals.

<div class="ojs-controls-wide">

### Controls

<div class="ojs-control-grid">

```{ojs}
viewof fText = Inputs.text({
  label: "Function f(x)",
  value: "x^3 - x - 2",
  placeholder: "Example: x^3 - x - 2, cos(x) - x"
})
```

```{ojs}
viewof a0 = Inputs.number({
  label: "Left endpoint a",
  value: 1,
  step: 0.1
})
```

```{ojs}
viewof b0 = Inputs.number({
  label: "Right endpoint b",
  value: 2,
  step: 0.1
})
```

```{ojs}
viewof tolerance = Inputs.number({
  label: "Tolerance",
  value: 1e-8,
  step: 1e-8
})
```

```{ojs}
viewof maxIterations = Inputs.range([1, 100], {
  label: "Maximum iterations",
  value: 40,
  step: 1
})
```

</div>

```{ojs}
viewof stepControl = {
  const div = html`
    <div class="ojs-button-row">
      <button class="ojs-next-button">Next iteration</button>
      <button class="ojs-reset-button">Reset</button>
      <span style="color:#475569;font-weight:600;"></span>
    </div>
  `

  const nextButton = div.querySelector(".ojs-next-button")
  const resetButton = div.querySelector(".ojs-reset-button")
  const label = div.querySelector("span")

  let value = 0
  div.value = value
  label.textContent = `Current step: ${value}`

  nextButton.onclick = () => {
    value = Math.min(value + 1, Number(maxIterations))
    div.value = value
    label.textContent = `Current step: ${value}`
    div.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input"))
  }

  resetButton.onclick = () => {
    value = 0
    div.value = value
    label.textContent = `Current step: ${value}`
    div.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input"))
  }

  return div
}
```

</div>

<div class="ojs-output-wide">

```{ojs}
mathjs = window.math
```

```{ojs}
cleanExpression = expr => {
  return String(expr)
    .trim()
    .replaceAll("π", "pi")
}
```

```{ojs}
compiledF = {
  try {
    return mathjs.compile(cleanExpression(fText))
  } catch (err) {
    return null
  }
}
```

```{ojs}
f = x => {
  if (!compiledF) return NaN

  try {
    const y = compiledF.evaluate({x})
    return Number(y)
  } catch (err) {
    return NaN
  }
}
```

```{ojs}
bisectionResult = {
  let a = Number(a0)
  let b = Number(b0)
  const tol = Number(tolerance)
  const maxIter = Number(maxIterations)

  if (!compiledF) {
    return {
      valid: false,
      statusType: "bad",
      message: "Could not parse the function. Try x^3 - x - 2, cos(x) - x, or exp(-x) - x.",
      rows: []
    }
  }

  if (!Number.isFinite(a) || !Number.isFinite(b)) {
    return {
      valid: false,
      statusType: "bad",
      message: "The endpoints must be finite numbers.",
      rows: []
    }
  }

  if (a === b) {
    return {
      valid: false,
      statusType: "bad",
      message: "The endpoints a and b must be different.",
      rows: []
    }
  }

  if (a > b) {
    const temp = a
    a = b
    b = temp
  }

  let fa = f(a)
  let fb = f(b)

  if (!Number.isFinite(fa) || !Number.isFinite(fb)) {
    return {
      valid: false,
      statusType: "bad",
      message: "Could not evaluate f(a) or f(b). Check the function and interval.",
      rows: []
    }
  }

  if (fa === 0) {
    return {
      valid: true,
      statusType: "good",
      message: `The left endpoint a = ${a} is already a root.`,
      rows: [{n: 0, a, b, m: a, fa, fb, fm: fa, width: b - a}]
    }
  }

  if (fb === 0) {
    return {
      valid: true,
      statusType: "good",
      message: `The right endpoint b = ${b} is already a root.`,
      rows: [{n: 0, a, b, m: b, fa, fb, fm: fb, width: b - a}]
    }
  }

  if (fa * fb > 0) {
    return {
      valid: false,
      statusType: "bad",
      message: `Bisection requires f(a) and f(b) to have opposite signs. Here f(a) = ${fa.toPrecision(6)} and f(b) = ${fb.toPrecision(6)}.`,
      rows: []
    }
  }

  const rows = []

  for (let n = 0; n <= maxIter; n++) {
    const m = 0.5 * (a + b)
    const fm = f(m)

    rows.push({
      n,
      a,
      b,
      m,
      fa,
      fb,
      fm,
      width: b - a
    })

    if (!Number.isFinite(fm)) {
      return {
        valid: false,
        statusType: "bad",
        message: `Stopped because f(m) is not finite at step ${n}.`,
        rows
      }
    }

    if (Math.abs(fm) < tol || Math.abs(b - a) < tol) {
      const last = rows[rows.length - 1]

      return {
        valid: true,
        statusType: "good",
        message: `Converged after ${n + 1} iteration${n + 1 === 1 ? "" : "s"}. Approximate root: m ≈ ${last.m.toPrecision(10)}.`,
        rows
      }
    }

    if (fa * fm < 0) {
      b = m
      fb = fm
    } else {
      a = m
      fa = fm
    }
  }

  const last = rows[rows.length - 1]

  return {
    valid: true,
    statusType: "warn",
    message: `Reached the maximum number of iterations. Last midpoint: m ≈ ${last.m.toPrecision(10)}, interval width = ${last.width.toExponential(3)}.`,
    rows
  }
}
```

```{ojs}
visibleRows = bisectionResult.rows.slice(
  0,
  Math.min(Number(stepControl), bisectionResult.rows.length - 1) + 1
)
```

```{ojs}
currentBisectionRow = {
  if (visibleRows.length === 0) return null
  return visibleRows[visibleRows.length - 1]
}
```

```{ojs}
bisectionXRange = {
  // const useAuto = autoRange.includes("Automatically adjust axes")

  // if (!useAuto) {
  //   let lo = Number(xminManual)
  //   let hi = Number(xmaxManual)

  //   if (!Number.isFinite(lo) || !Number.isFinite(hi) || lo === hi) {
  //     lo = Number(a0) - 1
  //     hi = Number(b0) + 1
  //   }

  //   if (lo > hi) {
  //     const temp = lo
  //     lo = hi
  //     hi = temp
  //   }

  //   return {lo, hi}
  // }

  const values = []

  const aStart = Number(a0)
  const bStart = Number(b0)

  if (Number.isFinite(aStart)) values.push(aStart)
  if (Number.isFinite(bStart)) values.push(bStart)

  for (const row of visibleRows) {
    if (Number.isFinite(row.a)) values.push(row.a)
    if (Number.isFinite(row.b)) values.push(row.b)
    if (Number.isFinite(row.m)) values.push(row.m)
  }

  let lo
  let hi

  if (values.length === 0) {
    lo = -5
    hi = 5
  } else {
    lo = Math.min(...values)
    hi = Math.max(...values)

    if (lo === hi) {
      lo = lo - 2
      hi = hi + 2
    }
  }

  const width = hi - lo
  const pad = Math.max(0.25 * width, 0.5)

  return {
    lo: lo - pad,
    hi: hi + pad
  }
}
```

```{ojs}
bisectionYRange = {
  const lo = bisectionXRange.lo
  const hi = bisectionXRange.hi
  const values = [0]

  const n = 500

  for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
    const x = lo + (hi - lo) * i / (n - 1)
    const y = f(x)

    if (Number.isFinite(y) && Math.abs(y) < 1e8) {
      values.push(y)
    }
  }

  for (const row of visibleRows) {
    if (Number.isFinite(row.fa) && Math.abs(row.fa) < 1e8) values.push(row.fa)
    if (Number.isFinite(row.fb) && Math.abs(row.fb) < 1e8) values.push(row.fb)
    if (Number.isFinite(row.fm) && Math.abs(row.fm) < 1e8) values.push(row.fm)
  }

  let ymin = Math.min(...values)
  let ymax = Math.max(...values)

  if (ymin === ymax) {
    ymin = ymin - 1
    ymax = ymax + 1
  }

  const height = ymax - ymin
  const pad = Math.max(0.15 * height, 0.5)

  return {
    lo: ymin - pad,
    hi: ymax + pad
  }
}
```

```{ojs}
bisectionPlot = {
  const div = html`<div class="plotly-box-large"></div>`

  const xlo = bisectionXRange.lo
  const xhi = bisectionXRange.hi
  const ylo = bisectionYRange.lo
  const yhi = bisectionYRange.hi

  const n = 700
  const xs = Array.from({length: n}, (_, i) => xlo + (xhi - xlo) * i / (n - 1))
  const ys = xs.map(x => f(x)).map(y => Number.isFinite(y) && Math.abs(y) < 1e8 ? y : null)

  const data = [
    {
      x: xs,
      y: ys,
      type: "scatter",
      mode: "lines",
      name: "f(x)",
      line: {
        color: "#2563eb",
        width: 3
      }
    },
    {
      x: [xlo, xhi],
      y: [0, 0],
      type: "scatter",
      mode: "lines",
      name: "y = 0",
      line: {
        color: "#111827",
        width: 2
      }
    }
  ]

  const shapes = []

  if (currentBisectionRow) {
    const row = currentBisectionRow

    shapes.push({
      type: "rect",
      xref: "x",
      yref: "paper",
      x0: row.a,
      x1: row.b,
      y0: 0,
      y1: 1,
      fillcolor: "rgba(37, 99, 235, 0.14)",
      line: {
        width: 0
      }
    })

    data.push({
      x: [row.a, row.b],
      y: [row.fa, row.fb],
      type: "scatter",
      mode: "markers+text",
      name: "Endpoints",
      text: ["a", "b"],
      textposition: "top center",
      marker: {
        color: "#2563eb",
        size: 11,
        line: {
          color: "white",
          width: 1
        }
      }
    })

    data.push({
      x: [row.m],
      y: [row.fm],
      type: "scatter",
      mode: "markers+text",
      name: "Midpoint",
      text: ["m"],
      textposition: "top center",
      marker: {
        color: "#dc2626",
        size: 13,
        line: {
          color: "white",
          width: 1
        }
      }
    })

    data.push({
      x: [row.a, row.a],
      y: [0, row.fa],
      type: "scatter",
      mode: "lines",
      name: "Endpoint guides",
      showlegend: false,
      line: {
        color: "#94a3b8",
        width: 1.5,
        dash: "dot"
      },
      hoverinfo: "skip"
    })

    data.push({
      x: [row.b, row.b],
      y: [0, row.fb],
      type: "scatter",
      mode: "lines",
      name: "Endpoint guides",
      showlegend: false,
      line: {
        color: "#94a3b8",
        width: 1.5,
        dash: "dot"
      },
      hoverinfo: "skip"
    })

    data.push({
      x: [row.m, row.m],
      y: [0, row.fm],
      type: "scatter",
      mode: "lines",
      name: "Midpoint guide",
      showlegend: false,
      line: {
        color: "#dc2626",
        width: 1.5,
        dash: "dot"
      },
      hoverinfo: "skip"
    })
  }

  const layout = {
    title: {
      text: "Bisection Method"
    },
    margin: {
      l: 65,
      r: 30,
      t: 60,
      b: 55
    },
    xaxis: {
      title: "x",
      range: [xlo, xhi],
      zeroline: true
    },
    yaxis: {
      title: "f(x)",
      range: [ylo, yhi],
      zeroline: true
    },
    shapes,
    legend: {
      orientation: "h",
      y: -0.18
    },
    hovermode: "closest"
  }

  Plotly.newPlot(div, data, layout, {
    responsive: true,
    displaylogo: false
  })

  return div
}
```

```{ojs}
bisectionStatus = {
  const cls =
    bisectionResult.statusType === "good"
      ? "ojs-status ojs-status-good"
      : bisectionResult.statusType === "bad"
        ? "ojs-status ojs-status-bad"
        : "ojs-status ojs-status-warn"

  return html`<div class="${cls}">
    <strong>Status:</strong> ${bisectionResult.message}
  </div>`
}
```

```{ojs}
bisectionTable = {
  const rows = visibleRows

  if (rows.length === 0) {
    return html`<div class="ojs-status">No iterations to display yet.</div>`
  }

  return html`
   <style>
   /* Adds clean horizontal spacing between your table columns */
   .ojs-table th, 
   .ojs-table td {
     padding: 8px 15px; 
     text-align: left;
   }
   </style>
    <div class="ojs-table-wrap">
      <table class="ojs-table">
        <thead>
          <tr>
            <th>n</th>
            <th>${tex`a_n`}</th>
            <th>${tex`b_n`}</th>
            <th>${tex`m_n`}</th>
            <th>${tex`f(a_n)`}</th>
            <th>${tex`f(b_n)`}</th>
            <th>${tex`f(m_n)`}</th>
            <th>width</th>
          </tr>
        </thead>
        <tbody>
          ${rows.map(row => html`
            <tr>
              <td>${row.n}</td>
              <td>${Number.isFinite(row.a) ? row.a.toPrecision(10) : "NaN"}</td>
              <td>${Number.isFinite(row.b) ? row.b.toPrecision(10) : "NaN"}</td>
              <td>${Number.isFinite(row.m) ? row.m.toPrecision(10) : "NaN"}</td>
              <td>${Number.isFinite(row.fa) ? row.fa.toExponential(5) : "NaN"}</td>
              <td>${Number.isFinite(row.fb) ? row.fb.toExponential(5) : "NaN"}</td>
              <td>${Number.isFinite(row.fm) ? row.fm.toExponential(5) : "NaN"}</td>
              <td>${Number.isFinite(row.width) ? row.width.toExponential(4) : "NaN"}</td>
            </tr>
          `)}
        </tbody>
      </table>
    </div>
  `
}
```

</div>

## Explanation

At each step, the bisection method computes the midpoint

$$
m=\frac{a+b}{2}.
$$

Then it keeps the half of the interval where the sign change still occurs. Repeating this process makes the interval smaller and smaller around the root.

## What the picture is showing

The blue curve is the graph of \(f(x)\). The black horizontal line is \(y=0\).

The shaded blue region is the current interval \([a,b]\). The blue points show the endpoint values \((a,f(a))\) and \((b,f(b))\).

The red point is the midpoint \((m,f(m))\). Each time you click **Next iteration**, the method halves the interval and keeps the side that still contains a sign change.

If **Automatically adjust axes** is selected, the plot range updates dynamically when you change the starting interval.
